page.text: This specification shall be followed for the fabrication of all types of welded joints of piping
system with in battery limits of the plant .
The welded pipe joints shall include the following:
a. All pipe joints, longitudinal butt weld (for Tanks / Plate works), circumferencial butt weld
b. Attachment of forging, flanges and other supports to pipe.
c. Welded manifold header and other sub assemblies.
d. Welded branch connections with or without RF Pads
e. Joints in welded /fabricated piping components
The attachment of smaller connections for vents/drains/drips/and other instrument
Any approval granted by Owner/CONSULTANT shall not relieve the Contractor of his
All welding works, equipment for welding, heat treatement, other auxillury function and the
welding personnel shall meet the requirement of latest edition of the following accepted
Welding Rods, Electrodes and Filler Metals.
In the event of any differences due to the additional requirements mentioned in this
specification over and above those obligatory as per code this specification shall be binding.
However in the event of any differences due to the additional requirements mentioned in any
applicable specification (Example:- Statutory, Licensor specification etc.) mentioned in the
tender, the most stringent requirement shall govern.
In general, use of carbon steel, alloy steel and stainless steel is envisaged. The details of the
material specifications are given in the welding specification chart.
The Contractor shall provide manufacturer test certificate for every heat number of the
The Contractor shall provide at his own expense all the welding consumables necessary for
the execution of the job such as electrodes, filler wire, argon etc. and this should bear approval
The welding electrode and filler wire supplied by the Contractor shall conform to the class
specified in the welding specification chart.
The Contractor shall submit batch test certificate from the electrode manufacturers giving
details of physical and chemical test for each batch of electrodes to be used.
All low hydrogen type of electrode shall be rebaked at 350 deg C for one hour minimum and
stored in ovens kept at 80 to 100 deg C before use or as per manufactur spec.
The electrodes, filler wires and flux used shall be free from rust, oil, grease, earth and other
foreign matter affect the quality of welding.
Tungsten electrodes used shall confirm to ASME Sec II C SFA 5.12 specification.
Argon gas used in GTAW welding for shielding purpose shall be 99.995% pure. The purity of
the gas shall be certified by the manufacturer. The rate of flow for shielding purpose shall be
Argon gas with a purity level of 99.995% shall be used for purging.
The purging shall be maintained during root pass and first filling pass to minimize oxidation
on the inner side of the pipe.
Initial purging shall be maintained for the sufficient period of time to remove trapped air. In no
case the initial purging period be should be less than 10 Min. High gas pressure should be
Dams used for conserving inert gas during purging shall be removed after completion of the
welding and shall be accounted for. Where ever removal of Dams is not possible, water
For socket type of welding purging is not required.
Welding of various materials under this specification shall be carried out using one or more
The welding process to be used are given in the welding specification chart. Any deviation
desired by the Contractor shall be approved by Owner / CONSULTANT.
Automatic and semi-automatic process shall be used only with the approval of Owner /
CONSULTANT. The welding procedure adopted and consumables used shall be specifically
6.4 Combination of different welding processes could be used for a particular joint only after duly
qualifying the welding procedure to be adopted and obtaining the approval of Owner /
Welding procedure qualification shall be carried out in accordance with the relevant
requirements of ASME Sec. IX latest edition or other applicable codes and the job
requirements. The Contractor shall submit the proposed welding procedure specifications
required for the execution of the job in format as per Section IX immediately after the receipt
of the order. Owner/CONSULTANT inspector will review, check and approve the proposed
welding procedure submitted and shall release the procedure for qualification Record (PQR)
tests. The procedure qualification test shall be carried out by the Contractor at his own
expense. A complete set of test results in the format as per Section IX shall be submitted to
the Owner/CONSULTANT inspector for his approval immediately after completing the
procedure qualification test and at least 2 weeks before the commencement of actual work.
Standard test as specified in the code shall be carried out in all cases. In addition to these
tests, other test like macro/micro examination, hardness test, dye penetrant examination,
charpy V-notch, Corrosion tests, impact tests etc. shall be carried out on specimens
depending upon the type of base material, operating conditions and requirements laid down
in the detailed drawings and specifications. It shall be the responsibility of the Contractor to
carry out all the tests required to the satisfaction of the Owner / Consultant inspector. Based
on the acceptance of PQR, WPS shall be prepared for Owner / Contractor approval.
Welders shall be qualified in accordance with the ASME Section-IX or other applicable
codes. The Owners inspector shall witness the test and certify the qualification of each
welder separately. Only those welders who have been approved by the Owner /
Consultant Inspector shall be employed for welding. Contractor shall submit the welder
qualification test reports in the format as per Section IX and obtain express approval
before commencement of work. It shall be the responsibility of Contractor to carry out
qualification tests of welders. For welding of the steam piping, falling under the purview
of Indian Boiler Regulations only those welders with IBR Certification, qualified by Boiler
Inspectorate, and acceptable to the local Boiler Inspector authority shall be employed.
The welders shall always have in their possession the identification card as issued by
Contractor and shall produce it on demand by the Owner / Consultant inspector. It shall
be the responsibility of the Contractor to issue the identity cards after it has been duly
No welder shall be permitted to work without the possession of identity card.
If a welder is found to perform a type of welding or in a position for which he is not
qualified, he shall be debarred from doing any further work. All welds performed by an
unqualified welder shall be cut and redone by a qualified welder at the expense of the
Welder performance record shall be made for each welder.
The edges to be welded shall be prepared to meet joint design requirement by any of the
a. Carbon Steel  Gas cutting, machining or grinding method shall be used.
b. Low alloy steel (Containing up to 5% chromium) - Gas cutting, machining or grinding
c. High Alloy Steel (> 5% chromium) and stainless steel, Nickel Alloy  Plasma cutting,
machining, or grinding method shall be used.
d. For tapping magnetic drill shall be used wherever it is possible to use.
The ends of the weld shall be properly clean to remove paint, oil, grease, rust, oxides, sand,
earth and other foreign matters. The end shall be dry before the welding commences.
Wire brush used for cleaning stainless steel joint shall have stainless steel wires and the
grinding wheel used for grinding stainless steel shall be of suitable type. Separate grinding
wheels and wire brushes should be used for carbon steel and stainless steel.
Components to be welded shall be aligned and spaced as per requirement laid down in
applicable code. Flame heating for adjustment and correction of ends is not permitted unless
Tack welds for maintaining the alignment of pipe joints shall be made only by qualified welders
using approved WPS. Defective tack welds must be removed prior to the actual welding of
the joints. Tacking cleats to be used in place of direct tacking (Pipe to Fittings / Pipe to Pipe )
and tacking clets shall be of same material as of pipe & fittings.
Tack weld should be equally spaced. Minimum number of tacks shall be
a. 3 Tacks  for 2 and smaller dia pipe
b. 4 tacks  for 3 to 12 dia pipes
c. 6 Tacks  for 14 and above larger pipes.
Welding shall commence only after approval of fit up by welding inspector.
The parts being welded and welding personnel should be adequately protected from rain and
strong winds. In the absence of such a protection no welding shall be carried out.
During field welding using GTAW process particular care shall be exercised to prevent any
a. Root pass shall be made with electrode / filler wires recommended in the welding
specification chart. Prefer size of electrode is 2.5 mm dia but in any case not less than 1.6
b. Upward technic shall be adopted for welding pipe held fixed with its axis horizontal.
c. The root pass of butt joint should be executed so as to achieve full penetration with
complete fusion of the root edges. Weld projection inside the pipe shall be as per
e. Penning shall not be used.
Golden / Closure / Tie-inn Joints : Root pass welding shall be with GTAW process only.
Root visual inspection with DPT/MPT is mandatory to perform.
a. Joint shall be completed using the class of electrode recommended in the approved
WPS. Size of electrode shall not exceed 4mm in dia for stainless steel and alloy steel
b. Butt joints shall be completed with a covered layer that would affect good fusion at the
joint edges and a gradual notch free surface.
c. Each weld joints shall have workmanship like finish. Weld identification work shall be
stamped clearly at each joint adjacent to the weld. Metal stamping shall not be used for
this pipe having wall thickness of less than 3.5mm. Suitable paint shall be used on thin
a. Where welds are to be produced between carbon steel and alloy steel, pre heat and
PWHT requirement shall be those specified for corresponding alloy steel material.
b. For welds between two dissimilar Cr.Mo. low alloy steel pre heat and PWHT shall be
those specified for higher alloy steel.
c. For carbon steel / alloy steel to stainless steel welds use of filler wire / electrodes E/ER-
309/E-310/ E NiCr Fe-3 shall be made.
d. The welding procedure, electrode filler wires to be used shall be approved by Owner /
a. No welding shall be performed without pre heating the joints to 10 deg C when the ambient
b. Preheating requirement of various materials shall be as per welding specification chart.
c. Preheating shall be performed using resistance or induction heating method. Preheating
by gas burner with neutral flame shall be carried out only if permitted by Owner /
d. Preheating shall be extended not less than 50mm on both sides of the weld.
e. Preheating temperature shall be maintained during welding.
pyrometers, shall be used to measure preheat and interpass temperatures.
g. Preheating for stainless steel is not recommended. Check of interpass temperature for
stainless steel shall be done by digital temperature gauge.
h. The preheat zone shall extend 75 mm or a distance equal to four (4) times the material
thickness, whichever is the greater, beyond each edge of the weld.
The maximum preheats and interpass temperature for carbon steel and low-alloy steel
The maximum interpass temperature for austenitic stainless steel shall be 175°C
Unless otherwise specified more stringently in other specifications (Example:- Statutory,
Licensor specification etc.) for alloy steel materials such as Cr.Mo. steel, if the PWHT is
not performed immediately after welding, the weld joint and adjacent portion of the pipe
at least 50mm on either side of the weld shall be uniformly heated to 315 deg C and
maintained for half an hour minimum. The joint shall than be wrapped in insulation before
allowing it to cool to room temperature.
a. PWHT shall be in accordance with the referenced code and additional requirements
of this specification. A written procedure describing the general PWHT requirements shall
be submitted for review and approval to Owner / Consultant. The PWHT procedure
shall include descriptions of equipment, method of heating, location and type of heating
elements, temperature measurement and thermocouple locations.
b. Prior to the start of PWHT, the Contractor shall prepare a table, listing each joint or
component requiring heat treatment and submit to Owner / Consultant for review. The
table shall include the following information for each joint or component: Location, drawing
number, diameter, wall thickness, material, heating rate, cooling rate, soak temperature
c. Heating and cooling rates shall conform to referenced code or standard or approved
d. PWHT shall follow all welding, including support clips, and structural parts and repairs,
but shall be performed prior to any hydro test or other load test.
e. PWHT shall be carried out using one (1) or more of the following types of heat sources:
Permanent or semi-permanent furnaces using gas or electric heaters. The furnace
atmosphere shall be selected to avoid excessive oxidation during the heat treatment.
Direct flame impingement by furnace burners or torch flames is prohibited.
Thermocouples and a calibrated temperature chart au t o recorder shall be used to
provide accurate and legible record of all PWHTs.
g. All thermocouple attachments shall be adequately insulated to avoid temperature
misreading caused by effect of radiation.
h. Temperature r ecor der s shall be calibrated ever y six ( 6) months and a r ecor d shall
be maintained on a recorder. The o r i g i n al calibration certificate shall be available for
Prior to the start of PWHT, components shall be checked to ensure that all restraints
are removed and the component is free to expand.
Surface to the component subject to a heat treatment shall be dry and free from oil,
k. All machined surfaces, such as flange faces, bolts holes, threads etc., shall be protected
from oxidation during the heat treatment.
All PWHT chart records shall be correctly identified and shall indicate as minimum:
The weld number and the corresponding thermocouple number with their location.
m. After completion of the PWHT, all thermocouples shall be removed and the attachment
areas shall be grounded smooth to clean sound metal and DPT to be carried out.
n. Welding or heating after the final PWHT is not permitted without approval of Owner /
o. The maximum variation in temperature within the heated zone shall not exceed 30°C
p. The temperature attained by the portion under heat treatment shall be recorded by
thermocouple. Adequate number of thermocouple should be attached to the pipe directly
at equally spaced location along the periphery of the pipe joint. The minimum number of
thermocouples attached per joint shall be
q. Automatic temperature recorder suitably calibrated should use for measuring and
Immediately on completion of heat treatment PWHT chart record alomngwith hardness
test result on the weld joints shall be submitted to Owner / Consultant for approval.
s. Each joint shall bear an identification number which shall be maintained in the piping
sketch / isometric and on corresponding PWHT chart.
Welding Procedure Specification is to be prepared, describing the low energy capacitor
discharge equipment, the combination of materials to be joined, and the technique of
application; qualification of the welding procedure is not required.
Weld hardness testing of production welds as required by ASME B31.3 shall be
performed in accordance with ASME B31.3.
Testing shall be conducted with portable hardness testers (Microdur or approved
Contractor shall document the results of all hardness tests for review by Owner/
All hardness readings in excess of values shown above shall be reported to Owner /
Consultant. Any corrective actions shall be reviewed with Owner / Consultant,
Non ferrous materials and austenitic stainless steel welds shall not require
hardness testing, regardless of the service.
If PWHT is applied, hardness testing shall be performed after PWHT.
The ferrite content of first two (2) austenitic stainless steel production welds made by
each welder using the SMAW process, shall be checked in the as welded condition
using a ferrite scope. The ferrite content shall be between 3% and 10%, unless specified
otherwise. Calibration of the magnetic instrument will conform to current AWS  A4.2
A minimum of six (6) ferrite readings will be taken on the surface to be tested.
Type 310 welds are exempt from this requirement.
Contractor shall implement a material control system to ensure that materials can be identified
and traced from source to their installed location and associated with the appropriate
certification. 100% colour code implementation for the material shall be ensured from vendor
shop for all piping materials. The methods of controlling the materials to be used and erected
at site are to be established and implemented at site. It includes requirements for receiving,
storing and releasing of all materials and equipment including the field purchased materials
Contractor supplied materials shall be with original, or verified copies of original certification.
Material Certificates shall be made available to Owner/Consultant for review.
Full traceability of piping materials shall be formally documented and maintained throughout
fabrication, painting, erection and test activities. Where piping materials have not been
maintained by traceability, Contractor shall undertake the necessary testing to demonstrate
that all the material used in the pressure loop is acceptable to specification, to the satisfaction
of the Owner / Consultant. Testing may include PMI and/or the chemical analysis and
mechanical testing of samples. Consultant / Owner has right to have any random material
sample tested at third party lab at contractor cost. 100% traceability is required for all lines
other than carbon steel within a specified temperature and pressure range.
All weld joint shall be free from adherent, weld spatters, slag, dirt or foreign matters. This can
be achieved by brushing / grinding. For stainless steel, brushes with stainless steel bristle
Suitable re-agent shall be applied for SS Loint cleaning.
Prior to star t of welding, inspection pr ocedures & NDT char t shall be established
in accor dance with reference code. A written procedure for each inspection method and
technique (including acceptance criteria) to be used shall be submitted to Owner /
Consultant. Inspection procedures in conformance with other standards are acceptable
only with approval of Owner / Consultant.
Non- destructive examinations (NDE) for final acceptance shall be performed after any
PWHT. Additional NDE may be performed by Contractor prior to PWHT to ensure all
reject able indications have been removed.
Surface irregular, including cap welds, inhibiting accurate interpretation of the specified
method of NDE shall be ground smooth.
The minimum extent of examination shall be based on the requirements of ASME B31.3
Random examination on a percentage basis, for example 5% of pipe welds, is
Examination is defined as 100% inspection of 5% of the production welds for each day
production made by each welder. The first two (2) production welds made, by each
welder, shall be included in the 5%. All welders, welding processes, pipe sizes and
materials shall be represented as a minimum. The examination shall be concurrent with
the fabrication. For each weld found to be defective, two (2) additional representative
welds shall be examined at contractors own cost. In case these additional welds are free
from defects, only the defects indicated in the first Examination shall be repaired and re-
examined. However, if either of the two (2) additional welds shows defects, all welds
Fully non-destructively examined and repaired as necessary, or
Any welder or welding operator having a weld rejection rate exceeding 10% of the
deposited weld length shall be disqualified from the site or be subject to 100% non-
destructive examination until the rejection rate reaches a level previously agreed upon
Non-destructive testing shall be performed by personnel qualified in accordance with
ASNT recommended practice SNT-TC -1A, minimum level 2 or approved equivalent.
Certification of personnel shall be made available by the Contractor. Contractor's
inspectors, responsible for the performance of visual inspections, shall be adequately
trained in both welding and inspection.
Inspection at the mill, shop or fabrication yard shall not release Contractor from
responsibility for repairing or replacing any defective material or workmanship that may
be subsequently discovered in the field.
i. All welds shall be visually inspected by Contractor after completion. Each weld shall
be uniform in width and size throughout its full length. In addition, the cover passes
(final weld layer) shall be free of course ripples, grooves, overlaps, arc strikes, abrupt
ridges and valleys. No undercutting is allowed.
Performance of the radiography examination of welds shall be in accordance with
Acceptance criteria shall be conforming the referenced code.
Radiography examination of welds in NPS 2 ½ " pipe or smaller shall be performed by
the elliptical projection technique. At least two (2) separate exposures are required at
If a pressure-containing weld will not be hydro tested, with prior approval of Owner /
Consultant, it shall be 100% radiography examined in accordance with the referenced
In case radiography examination is not practical, then other NDE method shall be specified.
Performance of the liquid penetrant examination of welds shall be in accordance with
Acceptance criteria shall conform the referenced code.
Performance of the magnetic particle examination of welds shall be in accordance with
Acceptance criteria shall be conforming the referenced code.
Permanent magnet yokes are not permitted.
Magnetic particle examination shall be performed on any structural attachments on
pressure-retaining components made of ferromagnetic material that are not hydro tested
Performance of the ultrasonic examination of welds shall be in accordance with the
Acceptance criteria shall be conforming the referenced code.
Ultrasonic testing may be substituted for radiography if approved by Owner / Consultant.
No welding joints shall be repaired more than twice. If the weld is still not acceptable
after the second repair, Owner / Consultant shall have the sole authority to permit
additional repair attempt or to require the entire weld be cut out.
Cracked welds shall be cut out and re-welded.
Repair welding shall be performed using a properly qualified and approved procedure.
In- process repairs (i.e. repairs performed prior to completion of the joint, using the same
welding procedure as for the original fabrication) during production does not require a
All repaired welds shall, as a minimum, be inspected using the original testing method.
Additional test methods may also be required, if deemed necessary by O w n er /
Damage to the base metal or welds, including dimensional changes caused by external
forces, requires special repair and inspection procedures to be submitted to Owner /
Consultant for review and approval prior to undertaking the repairs.
Any weld not meeting the acceptance criteria of the referenced code or standard shall
Repair of surface defects in welds by chipping, grinding and gouging are permitted
provided the minimum required wall thickness plus corrosion allowance is maintained.
Removal of defects shall be verified by magnetic particle or liquid penetrant examination.
Repair welding of minor defects, as defined below, shall be done by qualified welders
Major defects in welds and base materials, as defined in below table shall be repaired
only after a written procedure for each individual repair has been approved by Owner /
Consultant. The written procedure shall include a sketch detailing extent and location of
the defect, how defects will be removed, how removal will be verified, qualified welding
be used, subsequent heat treatment and non-destructive examination.
Weld build up to correct manufacturing
Lack of fusion and/or penetration etc. at
Edge defect greater than 20% of edge
Note: * All weld build up or buttering shall be 100% examined by MT, followed by RT or UT.

text.b: DOCUMENTS TO BE SUBMITTED BY CONTRACTOR (4 COPIES EACH)

page.text: Batch test Certificates, for the electrodes used, obtained from the Electrode
Welding Procedure Specifications as per ASME Section-IX immediately after receipt of
Welding Procedure Qualification records as per ASME Section-IX.
Welder Performance Qualification records as per ASME Section-IX immediately after
Radiography Procedure and other NDT procedures.
Radiography test Report along with Radiography and other NDT reports.
Followings are the welding specification for guidelines / reference purpose only. Specific requirement
like welding specification, preheating, post heating, PWHT, NDT etc. as per applicable codes and
ASTM A 182 GR.F11 CL.2, ASTM A 234 GR.WP11 CL.1
CONTINUITY OF WELDING AND PREHEAT : INNERT PASS TEMP 250
C DURING WELD SHALL BE MAINTAINED
HOLDING TIME: : 1 HOUR PER INCH THK
1. POST WELD HEAT TREATMENT AND MAX. HARDNESS IS NOT MANDATORY ONLY WHEN THE UTS OF THE MATERIAL <=71,000 PSI AND THK <= 12.7MM
1. POST WELD HEAT TREATMENT SHALL BE DONE AS PER ASME SEC-IX & IBR REQUIREMENT FOR ALL THICKNESS
ASTM A 182 GR.F11 CL.2, ASTM A 234 GR.WP11 CL.1
CONTINUITY OF WELDING AND PREHEAT : INNERT PASS TEMP 250
C DURING WELD SHALL BE MAINTAINED
HOLDING TIME: : 1 HOUR PER INCH THK
